data.reasoning: Both models failed to review PKGBUILD (misclassified as binary), preventing analysis of build/install logic. No actual security assessment occurred.
coverage_issues: Critical gap: PKGBUILD skipped by both models due to erroneous binary classification. This file defines all build/install steps and hooks.
No other files (like .install scripts) were reviewed due to initial PKGBUILD failure.
false_negatives: All potential risks missed: No analysis of package()/build() functions, source URLs, or install hooks. Could have hidden unsafe commands (e.g., curl | bash, insecure permissions).
learnings: Fix binary detection: PKGBUILD is always text. Tool must never skip it.
Improve error handling: Abort audit cleanly when PKGBUILD fails to parse with actionable diagnostics.
Prioritization: PKGBUILD must be reviewed first; its analysis dictates which other files (install hooks) are relevant.
Model calibration: Train models to recognize AUR conventions (e.g., PKGBUILD syntax isn't 'binary').
